headlineSummary: HN discussion is broadly neutral, with Google Gemini and OpenAI in a close tie on limited evidence and Anthropic also landing neutral but with more mixed feedback, including both praise and criticism [E3] [E1] [E2].

ranking.summary: Google Gemini is neutral on limited evidence, with one negative comment focused on missing per-key spending limits for the API [E3].
OpenAI is neutral on limited evidence, with one negative comment criticizing heavy reliance on it for coding as a risky gamble [E1].
Anthropic is neutral with mixed feedback: one comment strongly praises Claude Code and Opus productivity, while another warns that depending on Anthropic for coding is a dangerous gamble [E2] [E1].

evidence.excerpt: The dependency we have with anthropic and openai for coding for instance is insane. Most accept it because either they don't care, or they just hope chinese will never stop open weights. The business model of open weights is very new, include some power play between countries and labs, and move an absurd amount of money without any concrete oversight from most people. It's a very dangerous gamble.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Anthropic and says dependence on it for coding is 'insane' and 'a very dangerous gamble,' which is clear criticism of relying on the provider.
The comment explicitly names OpenAI and frames dependence on it for coding as 'insane' and 'a very dangerous gamble,' indicating negative sentiment toward that reliance.

evidence.excerpt: Last October, I had a real epiphany using Claude Code at work. Suddenly, that initial inertia of starting something whether it’s drafting a JIRA ticket, structuring a PR, or just brainstorming completely vanished. I started using Claude exclusively in plan mode, and within minutes, I’d have full clarity on exactly what I wanted to do and how to do it. With the release of the Opus model, I felt 100% more productive because I stopped spending time on menial tasks like manual coding or documentation.

annotations.rationale: The excerpt explicitly references Claude Code and Opus and strongly praises their impact, describing an 'epiphany,' vanished inertia, and feeling '100% more productive.'

evidence.excerpt: Haven't touched gemini api since they did not support having a $ limit per api key. Is it possible now?

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly mentions the Gemini API and criticizes its lack of per-key spending limits, expressing dissatisfaction with billing/spend-control features.
